title: hibbertia fasciculiflora
intro: Hibbertia fasciculiflora, also known as the yellow daisy bush, is a small, shrub-like plant native to Australia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The leaves and flowers of the Hibbertia fasciculiflora plant are used in herbal preparations to treat various health issues, such as skin conditions, wounds, and infections. In herbal preparations, Hibbertia fasciculiflora is often combined with other plants to create teas, tinctures, and salves that are used to promote healing and prevent infection.
